req.title: Consent management
req.statement: A patient shall grant or revoke consent for data sharing; revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes.
error_pathways: accesses after revoke within SLA denied

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-015 mandates two primary behaviours: (1) a patient can grant or revoke consent via POST/PUT to /Consent and the record is persisted; (2) after revocation, downstream access must be denied within a 15-minute SLA window. Test design covers: EP – a well-formed grant request succeeds (positive partition); NEG – accessing a protected resource after revocation is denied; BVA – a data-access attempt made exactly at the 15-minute boundary (t=900 s) is still denied, while t=901 s is outside SLA (boundary between enforcement window edges); STC – the full state machine grant→revoke→access traces all consent state transitions. Each test is idempotent through unique patient fixture teardown and does not rely on shared state.

tests.name: test_grant_consent_stores_record
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
No prior Consent resource exists for this patient-subject pair
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a valid FHIR Consent resource payload with status=active and provision.type=permit
POST the payload to /Consent with Authorization: Bearer <token>
Assert HTTP 201 Created
GET the returned resource URL and verify stored status
expected: HTTP 201 returned with a resource id in the response body
Subsequent GET /Consent/<id> returns status=active and provision.type=permit
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests, time
from tests.fixtures import patient_auth_token, base_url, cleanup_consent

def test_grant_consent_stores_record(patient_auth_token, base_url, cleanup_consent):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {patient_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Consent",
        "status": "active",
        "scope": {"coding": [{"code": "patient-privacy"}]},
        "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "59284-0"}]}],
        "provision": {"type": "permit"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Consent", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Consent"
    resource_id = body["id"]
    cleanup_consent(resource_id)
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Consent/{resource_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    stored = get_resp.json()
    assert stored["status"] == "active"
    assert stored["provision"]["type"] == "permit"

tests.name: test_access_denied_after_revocation
preconditions: Patient has an existing active Consent resource granting data sharing
Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
actions: PUT /Consent/<id> with status=inactive (revocation) and provision.type=deny
Assert HTTP 200 on revocation
Immediately attempt GET /Patient/<subject_id>/$everything with patient token
Assert the access attempt is denied
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome indicating consent denied
trace: R-HLT-015: error pathway 'accesses after revoke within SLA denied'

tests.name: test_access_denied_at_exactly_15_minute_boundary
preconditions: Patient has an active Consent resource
System clock accessible; test environment allows time manipulation via <freeze_time> helper or system supports mocked elapsed time
actions: Record revocation timestamp T0 by revoking consent via PUT /Consent/<id>
Simulate or wait until elapsed time equals exactly 900 seconds (T0 + 15 min)
Attempt GET /Patient/<id>/$everything at T0+900 s
Assert access is still denied (boundary is inclusive)
Simulate or advance to T0+901 s and confirm SLA window closed (enforcement no longer guaranteed per requirement)
expected: At T0+900 s exactly, GET /Patient/<id>/$everything returns 401 or 403
At T0+901 s, the requirement SLA window has elapsed; behaviour outside mandatory enforcement
trace: R-HLT-015: 'enforced within 15 minutes' — boundary at exactly 900 s post-revocation
R-HLT-015: BVA on the 15-minute enforcement deadline

tests.name: test_grant_revoke_state_transition
preconditions: No Consent resource exists for the patient
actions: State: NO_CONSENT — POST /Consent with status=active, provision=permit → State: GRANTED
Verify GET /Patient/<id>/$everything returns 200 while consent is active
State: GRANTED — PUT /Consent/<id> with status=inactive, provision=deny → State: REVOKED
Verify GET /Patient/<id>/$everything returns 401/403 after revocation
State: REVOKED — PUT /Consent/<id> with status=active, provision=permit → State: GRANTED again
Verify GET /Patient/<id>/$everything returns 200 after re-grant
expected: Each state transition returns expected HTTP status on /Consent
Resource access mirrors consent state at every transition
trace: R-HLT-015: 'grant or revoke consent' — full state machine coverage
R-HLT-015: 'consent stored; downstream caches invalidated' on each transition
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests
from tests.fixtures import patient_auth_token, base_url, new_patient_subject
from tests.helpers import wait_for_cache_invalidation

def test_grant_revoke_state_transition(patient_auth_token, base_url, new_patient_subject):
    subject_id = new_patient_subject
    hdrs = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {patient_auth_token}",
            "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    def consent_body(status, ptype, rid=None):
        b = {"resourceType": "Consent", "status": status,
             "scope": {"coding": [{"code": "patient-privacy"}]},
             "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "59284-0"}]}],
             "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{subject_id}"},
             "provision": {"type": ptype}}
        if rid:
            b["id"] = rid
        return b
    r1 = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Consent", json=consent_body("active", "permit"), headers=hdrs)
    assert r1.status_code == 201
    rid = r1.json()["id"]
    wait_for_cache_invalidation()
    assert requests.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{subject_id}/$everything", headers=hdrs).status_code == 200
    r2 = requests.put(f"{base_url}/Consent/{rid}", json=consent_body("inactive", "deny", rid), headers=hdrs)
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    wait_for_cache_invalidation()
    assert requests.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{subject_id}/$everything", headers=hdrs).status_code in (401, 403)
    r3 = requests.put(f"{base_url}/Consent/{rid}", json=consent_body("active", "permit", rid), headers=hdrs)
    assert r3.status_code == 200
    wait_for_cache_invalidation()
    assert requests.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{subject_id}/$everything", headers=hdrs).status_code == 200

tests.name: test_consent_decision_table_permit_vs_deny
preconditions: Two patients: patient_A has active permit consent, patient_B has active deny consent
Authenticated tokens available for both patients
actions: GET /Patient/<patient_A_id>/$everything with patient_A token (permit in effect)
GET /Patient/<patient_B_id>/$everything with patient_B token (deny in effect)
Compare HTTP status codes against decision table
trace: R-HLT-015: 'grant or revoke consent' — decision table: active+permit=allow vs active+deny=block
R-HLT-015: error pathway 'accesses after revoke within SLA denied'
